coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should show NEPA review through the EA purpose-and-need framing or equivalent environmental assessment evidence.
coverage_items.obligation_area: National Environmental Policy Act applies

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should disclose environmental information and analysis needed for informed agency decision-making.
coverage_items.obligation_area: NEPA section 4332 environmental information is addressed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should support use of an EA by addressing whether significant effects are expected or whether an EIS is required.
coverage_items.obligation_area: NEPA level of review is supported

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should document review management, schedule, and unified-review considerations where they are part of the NEPA record.
coverage_items.obligation_area: NEPA timing and unified-review duties are addressed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should use 7 CFR part 1b as the controlling USDA NEPA procedural baseline.
coverage_items.obligation_area: 7 CFR part 1b - National Environment Policy Act is reviewed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should show NEPA review is integrated into agency decision-making and managed consistently and predictably.
coverage_items.obligation_area: 7 CFR § 1b.1 - Purpose is reviewed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should support threshold and level-of-review determinations under current USDA NEPA policy.
coverage_items.obligation_area: 7 CFR § 1b.2 - Policy is reviewed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should describe the proposal, alternatives as appropriate, affected environment, and environmental effects.
coverage_items.obligation_area: USDA EA requirements are met

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: A FONSI or decision notice should be traceable to the EA analysis and mitigation or design features relied on for the finding.
coverage_items.obligation_area: USDA FONSI support is traceable

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should document coordination and efficient environmental review practices where relevant to the project record.
coverage_items.obligation_area: Efficient and integrated environmental review is documented

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should use current definitions and flag superseded terminology when it appears as a mandatory legal standard.
coverage_items.obligation_area: 7 CFR § 1b.11 - Definitions and acronyms is reviewed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should reflect the current USDA NEPA rule baseline and transition context.
coverage_items.obligation_area: USDA final rule: National Environmental Policy Act, 91 FR 17092, Apr. 3, 2026 is reviewed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: Forest Service NEPA references should align with the current USDA NEPA rule and not rely on removed 36 CFR part 220 requirements as controlling law.
coverage_items.obligation_area: Forest Service Environmental Planning / NEPA page is reviewed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: Effects analysis should stay within the project-at-hand and reasonably foreseeable effects standard.
coverage_items.obligation_area: Seven County Infrastructure Coalition v. Eagle County, Supreme Court opinion is reviewed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should identify the decision, FONSI, or authorization that supplies final agency action where reviewability matters.
coverage_items.obligation_area: Administrative Procedure Act final agency action, 5 U.S.C. § 704 is reviewed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: Projects and activities authorized by the Forest Service should be consistent with applicable land management plan direction.
coverage_items.obligation_area: NFMA forest-plan consistency is addressed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should address land-management-plan components and project consistency under the planning rule.
coverage_items.obligation_area: 36 CFR part 219 - Planning is reviewed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The project approval document should describe consistency with applicable plan components under 36 CFR 219.15.
coverage_items.obligation_area: 36 CFR 219.15 project consistency is addressed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should document notice, comment, objection eligibility, and objection-resolution status where predecisional review applies.
coverage_items.obligation_area: 36 CFR part 218 - Project-level predecisional administrative review is reviewed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should verify cited Forest Service directives against current manuals, handbooks, and directive portals.
coverage_items.obligation_area: Forest Service National Directives portal is reviewed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should verify cited Region 1 supplements and field directives against current regional sources.
coverage_items.obligation_area: Forest Service Region 1 field directives index is reviewed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should document listed, sensitive, or conservation species reviews under current Forest Service species policy where biological resources are present.
coverage_items.obligation_area: FSM 2670 - Threatened, Endangered and Sensitive Plants and Animals is reviewed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should show that purpose, need, alternatives, and effects are consistent with multiple-use and sustained-yield management.
coverage_items.obligation_area: Multiple-Use Sustained-Yield Act, 16 U.S.C. §§ 528-531 is reviewed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should support national forest purposes, including watershed protection and forest-resource management where relevant.
coverage_items.obligation_area: Organic Administration Act, 16 U.S.C. § 475 is reviewed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should support use restrictions, special uses, closure/protection measures, or other occupancy/use controls where they are part of the decision.
coverage_items.obligation_area: Organic Administration Act, 16 U.S.C. § 551 is reviewed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should identify FLPMA Section 206 land-exchange authority and address public-interest, same-state, equal-value, cash-equalization, appraisal, and exchange-processing requirements where a federal land exchange is proposed.
coverage_items.obligation_area: FLPMA Section 206 land-exchange requirements are addressed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should use current Regional Forester species-of-conservation-concern information when plan consistency or biological resources are reviewed.
coverage_items.obligation_area: Northern Region species of conservation concern page is reviewed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: Check age of programmatic review, reevaluation if older than 5 years, and substantial new circumstances/information
coverage_items.obligation_area: 42 U.S.C. § 4336b - Programmatic environmental documents is reviewed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: When CE adoption or a categorical-exclusion path is actually implicated, the EA package should document the adopted CE or categorical-exclusion pathway and why EA review is appropriate.
coverage_items.obligation_area: 42 U.S.C. § 4336c - Adoption of categorical exclusions is reviewed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: Sponsor-prepared or applicant-funded materials should have disclosure and agency-responsibility documentation when present.
coverage_items.obligation_area: 42 U.S.C. § 4336f - Sponsor-prepared environmental documents is reviewed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should document a CE/FANEC screen, categorical-exclusion path, or extraordinary-circumstances review before this rule is applicable.
coverage_items.obligation_area: 7 CFR § 1b.3 - Categorical exclusions and FANEC is reviewed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should document subcomponent CE or USDA CE screening, including extraordinary-circumstances review, before this rule is applicable.
coverage_items.obligation_area: 7 CFR § 1b.4 - USDA subcomponent categorical exclusions is reviewed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: Applicant or third-party documentation should be independently evaluated and adopted by the Forest Service before supporting the EA.
coverage_items.obligation_area: 7 CFR § 1b.10 - Documentation prepared by applicant or third party is reviewed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should identify appeal rights, exhaustion, and effective-date handling where written authorizations are part of the decision.
coverage_items.obligation_area: 36 CFR part 214 - Postdecisional administrative review for written instruments is reviewed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should distinguish site-specific decisions from directive changes that require notice and comment.
coverage_items.obligation_area: 36 CFR part 216 - Notice and comment for Forest Service directives is reviewed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should identify and apply the forest plan that governs the project area.
coverage_items.obligation_area: 2022 Custer Gallatin Land Management Plan is applied

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should address ESA section 7 consultation when listed species or critical habitat may be affected.
coverage_items.obligation_area: Endangered Species Act section 7 consultation is addressed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should identify the consultation path and effects determinations when ESA consultation is implicated.
coverage_items.obligation_area: ESA consultation regulations are addressed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should address section 106 consultation and findings when historic properties may be affected.
coverage_items.obligation_area: NHPA section 106 compliance is addressed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should trace SHPO or THPO consultation, effects findings, and mitigation when 36 CFR part 800 applies.
coverage_items.obligation_area: 36 CFR part 800 consultation process is addressed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should document Montana SHPO review, concurrence, or consultation when cultural resources are implicated in Montana.
coverage_items.obligation_area: Montana SHPO review is addressed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should address roadless-area effects and consistency where inventoried roadless areas are implicated.
coverage_items.obligation_area: Roadless Rule compliance is addressed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should address wetlands protection, avoidance, minimization, or compensating measures when wetlands are implicated.
coverage_items.obligation_area: Executive Order 11990 wetlands protection is addressed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should address migratory bird effects or conservation measures when migratory birds may be affected.
coverage_items.obligation_area: Migratory Bird Treaty Act compliance is addressed

coverage_items.expected_package_evidence: The EA package should address agency responsibilities to protect migratory birds when they are implicated.
coverage_items.obligation_area: Executive Order 13186 migratory-bird responsibilities are addressed
